description_long: This is a large, multi-wing apartment building complex with a T-shaped footprint, featuring a central high-rise tower flanked by two lower, elongated wings. The facade is clad in light beige and gray concrete panels, punctuated by rows of identical windows and numerous balconies, giving the building a repetitive, modular appearance. The roof surfaces are flat and dark, likely made of tar or roofing felt, with the central tower rising significantly above the side wings. The balconies are distributed regularly along the exterior, each with metal railings, and the windows are symmetrically aligned in vertical and horizontal rows. The building's exterior suggests a utilitarian, Soviet-era or post-Soviet architectural style, emphasizing function over ornamentation. The structure is likely constructed from reinforced concrete, with glass windows and metal balcony railings, and is designed to accommodate a large number of residents.
description: A large, T-shaped apartment building complex with a tall central tower and two lower wings, featuring repetitive windows and balconies in a utilitarian style.
description_view_0: The central tower is prominent, flanked by two lower wings; the left wing's roof and balconies are clearly visible.
description_view_1: The building's full T-shaped layout is visible, with the central tower at the back and both wings extending forward; the flat roofs dominate this view.
description_view_2: The central tower is again central, but the right wing's roof and balconies are more visible, mirroring the left side of view 0.
description_view_3: The front facade of the building is shown, with the lower wing in the foreground and the central tower rising behind; balconies and windows are most visible here.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are arranged in regular, evenly spaced vertical and horizontal rows, creating a highly symmetrical and repetitive pattern across all facades.
